criteria.criterion_text: The review uses a representative sample.
criteria.guideline: A representative sample is a sufficiently large sample which includes records for all practitioners providing patient care and all types of care provided. The review should be conducted by clinical professionals authorised to make entries in the patient record.

criteria.criterion_text: Records comply with professionally acceptable norms (including legal requirements where applicable) relating to signature, use of abbreviations and legibility.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Standardised diagnosis and procedure codes are used.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be assessed by comparing the codes used in the service with country-specific directives. The responsible department should therefore have both the directives and the service’s coding system available for comparison.

criteria.criterion_text: Symbols and definitions are standardised.
FACILITIES, EQUIPMENT AND CONTRACTED SERVICES
OVERVIEW OF  FACILITIES, EQUIPMENT AND CONTRACTED SERVICES

Laws, regulations and inspections by national governmental and local authorities determine in large part how a facility is designed, used and maintained. All hospice and palliative care services, regardless of their size and resources, must comply with these requirements as part of their responsibilities to patients, families and personnel. The organisation must begin by complying with relevant laws and regulations. Over time they become more knowledgeable about the details of the physical facility they occupy. They begin to gather data proactively and carry out strategies to reduce risks and enhance the patient care environment.
Buildings, grounds, utilities and equipment are maintained and do not pose hazards to the occupants. The personnel providing the maintenance service are knowledgeable and competent.

The organisation needs to have the necessary equipment for comprehensive hospice and palliative care. To meet the standards, equipment must be in good working order. There is a wide range of equipment that the organisation may need in order to provide services which meet national and local needs, serve the nature of the service and support any procedures that the service performs.

Where service contracts/agreements are awarded to outside agencies, the hospice and palliative care service must ensure that there is a written contractual agreement outlining the service and standard of service to be delivered. Contracted agencies must undertake to provide services in accordance with infection control and health and safety requirements. Where applicable, the contracted personnel receive training with regard to waste disposal and infection control if this has not been undertaken to a satisfactory level by the contracted company.
Standards

standards.standard_title: Measures are in place to ensure that patient access to the hospice and palliative care service is facilitated by adequate infrastructural arrangements.
standards.intent: Access to care is the most fundamental right for potential patients. The service should develop strategies to overcome barriers to access to care, thereby improving their services for the catchment population.

criteria.criterion_text: Directional signs to the service are clearly readable and up to date.
criteria.guideline: This requirement ensures that members of the public, both locals and visitors, who are not familiar with the location of the service will be able to find it when necessary.

criteria.criterion_text: A telephone/emergency number is available and provided to patients on registration and on request.
criteria.guideline: This can be in the form of a business card or patient information leaflet. Where the service does not provide emergency services the emergency number must reflect the closest emergency facility to the palliative care service.

criteria.criterion_text: A phone number for the after-hours service is clearly displayed on the outside of the facility.
criteria.guideline: Where the service does not offer an after-hours service, an emergency number must be displayed. Compliance will be verified by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Parking is provided close to the building entrance for patients, including the physically challenged.
criteria.guideline: Surveyors will make a judgement based on the size of the facility and services provided.

criteria.criterion_text: There is wheelchair access to and within the building.
Ramps and stairs include safety features such as rails.
criteria.guideline: The provision of such safety features will be assessed during the visit to each hospice and palliative care service.

standards.standard_title: Functional facilities are available to provide safety and comfort for patients, personnel and other visitors.
standards.intent:
In order to provide safe patient care, each facility requires adequate resources. The building is appropriate for a healthcare service in terms of size and layout.
The physical facilities required include adequate office accommodation for personnel, a designated sluice area which is hygienically clean at all times, treatment and dressing rooms. Cleaning equipment is safely stored in a room or cupboard used for this purpose only. There are adequate toilet facilities for the number of patients and staff as determined by country-specific legislation. There is adequate lighting and ventilation.
Each consulting room, which may include an attached examination room/area:
Is free from excessive noise
Has adequate lighting
Is maintained at a comfortable ambient temperature
Ensures patient privacy when the patient needs to undress for a clinical examination (for example the use of adequate curtains or screens and gowns or sheets)
Buildings and grounds are maintained and do not pose hazards to the occupants. The construction of the building in terms of walls, ceilings, floors, doors and windows must be sound. The general appearance will be examined for neatness, condition of paintwork, signs of leakage, mould spots etc.

criteria.criterion_text: Laws, regulations and other requirements applicable to the organisation’s facilities are implemented and are available to the personnel.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  A copy of the occupation certificate issued in terms of the Building Control Act and regulations must be available during the survey. Where the practice is in a rented facility, a copy must be obtained from the landlord.  Copies of the laws, regulations and other requirements applicable to the structure of the facility should be available and accessible to personnel in either hard copy or electronic format and should include at least the Building Control Act and regulations.

criteria.criterion_text: The building is appropriate as a healthcare facility in terms of size and lay-out.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: The lay-out of the facility allows for effective flow of patient care.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: The waiting area is sufficient to accommodate the usual number of patients and other people who could be waiting at any given time.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation and by examination of daily patient attendance figures.

criteria.criterion_text: The waiting area caters for the specific safety needs of children, where applicable.
criteria.guideline: This includes for example, no open electrical sockets, measure to prevent children leaving the facility unnoticed, measures to prevent injury from inappropriate toys (ingestion of small objects, sharp protrusions, easily cleanable), measures to secure hazardous substances etc.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a designated space that offers privacy for every member of the interdisciplinary team working in the service at any time.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: All areas of the facility, including consultation rooms are clean, well ventilated and well maintained.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Sufficient office/administrative space is available for the personnel.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Toilet/washroom facilities are clean and in working order.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Separate sanitary facilities are provided for personnel.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a separate, secure area for personnel with adequate secure storage facilities for outdoor clothing, handbags and personal possessions.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Required furniture is available according to established lists and functioning properly.
criteria.guideline: The service must compile and maintain an inventory of furniture which must be available at the survey. As a minimum, a hospice and palliative care service must have at least the following: Chairs (in the waiting area, for administrative personnel, for clinical personnel and for patients and families in the treatment areas) Desks/workstations (sufficient for the size of the service) Examination bed (in each examination area) Lockable fire proof file cabinets Lockable fire proof cabinet (for all confidential documentation) Refuse bins for domestic and clinical waste in each functional area where appropriate.

criteria.criterion_text: Hand washing facilities, including water, soap and paper towels are available for patients and personnel.

standards.standard_title: Medical equipment is available and properly maintained to meet the needs of the patient population.
standards.intent:
Hospice and palliative care services are responsible for ensuring that appropriate medical equipment is available and ready for use at all times. There is an accountable, systematic approach to ensuring that cost-effective, safe and appropriate medical equipment is available to meet the demands of quality patient care.
The service manager takes responsibility for ensuring that medical equipment is available, appropriately maintained and calibrated and that the relevant personnel are competent to use it.
Each service ensures that it has the required equipment for comprehensive primary care and the level of services provided. The following is recommended:
Auroscope
Blood glucose monitoring equipment
Diagnostic Set
Disposable syringes and needles
Examination light
Gloves (sterile and non-sterile)
Height measurement device
Measuring tape
Monofilament for sensation testing
Nebulising equipment and supplies
Oxygen
Patella hammer
Peak flow meter
Scales
Spacer for inhaler
Specimen collection equipment
Sphygmomanometer with small medium and large cuffs
Stethoscope
Surgical masks
Thermometer
Torch
Tourniquet
Urine testing strips

criteria.criterion_text: Implemented policies and procedures guide the management of medical equipment.
criteria.guideline: Policies and procedures that detail how equipment is to be used, maintained and repaired and the level of personnel training required to use the equipment are implemented.

criteria.criterion_text: A designated individual supervises the management of medical equipment in the service.
criteria.guideline: The responsibilities of this individual include as a minimum: Ensuring that the required medical equipment as listed in the standard intent above is available in the service Audits on available medical equipment (for example to ensure that it is correctly used, calibrated, stored etc.) Compiling an inventory of medical equipment, which includes description, make, manufacturer, model, serial number, tracing number, date of purchase, purchase price, list of accessories, supplier details, guarantee expiry dates and commissioning information or certificates Regular inspections of medical equipment Testing of medical equipment, as appropriate to its use and requirements Ensuring planned preventative maintenance of equipment

criteria.criterion_text: There is an inventory of the medical equipment available in the service.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: The supply of medical equipment is adequate to meet the needs of the service.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified by observation and during personnel and patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Records are kept of the checking and maintenance of medical equipment.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented procedure known to the personnel for reporting defects in medical equipment.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured during personnel interviews and document review.

standards.standard_title: Facility maintenance is managed to ensure the provision of a safe and effective service.
standards.intent: Management ensures that sufficient, competent personnel are available to manage routine and emergency functions and meet the needs of a safe and effective health service. Personnel may be in the employ of the hospice and palliative care service or be contracted out. Where there are contracted personnel, there must be clearly specified contracts, outlining their roles and responsibilities.

criteria.criterion_text: Written policies and procedures guide service personnel on the implementation of all maintenance service requirements.
criteria.guideline: This criterion is assessed based on the evidence of effective maintenance found throughout the service during the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC, the transgressions need to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC/NC rating and should be based on accurate facts.

criteria.criterion_text: A designated, competent individual is responsible for supervising the maintenance of buildings, grounds and utilities.
criteria.guideline: If this role is fulfilled by the service manager or solo practitioner, compliance will be measured on evidence of effective maintenance of the buildings, grounds and utilities. Where a designated individual has been identified to fulfil this role, compliance will be measured in accordance with the requirements set out in the employee’s position description.

criteria.criterion_text: Where these services are outsourced the, personnel have access to a list of these private contractors/service providers with their contact numbers at all times.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured on documented evidence and during personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Written agreements ensure technical back-up services are available at all times during the opening or operational hours of the service.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: Basic maintenance equipment, tools and spare parts are available.
criteria.guideline: The type and number of items available will vary in each service according to the size of the facility and the services offered but should be suitable and sufficient to prevent unnecessary risk to patients, visitors and personnel or cessation of services due to deficiencies that are easily rectifiable.

standards.standard_title: The hospice and palliative care service implement a documented preventative planned maintenance programme for buildings, grounds and utilities.
standards.intent:
The service plans for regular in-house inspection of facilities to avoid hazards.
Building maintenance includes the monitoring of the following aspects:
The general appearance of the inside and outside structure which includes the construction of walls, floors, doors and windows
The condition of the paintwork
Water leaks, mould spots
Electrical wiring, for example exposed wires, switches, electrical sockets
Maintenance of the grounds (no litter, neat garden and grass kept short)

criteria.criterion_text: The service plans and budgets for the upgrading or replacing of systems, buildings or components needed for the continued operation of a safe and effective facility.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided for example in the budget and/or strategic plan.

criteria.criterion_text: The service has a documented preventative maintenance management plan in place.
criteria.guideline: Where the service is a tenant, parts of the preventative maintenance plan may be the function of the landlord. There must however still be documented evidence provided to the surveyors to demonstrate that the landlord has a preventative maintenance plan for the facility. This will allow the surveyors to assess the responsibilities of the landlord and the responsibilities of the service.

criteria.criterion_text: Documented inspections of the buildings and grounds are conducted at regular intervals as determined by policy.
criteria.guideline: The policy must include at least a) – e) in the standard intent above. Documented evidence will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented procedure known to the personnel for reporting defects.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured during personnel interviews and availability of the procedure.

standards.standard_title: Information and communication technology (ICT) equipment is available and properly maintained to meet the needs of the service.
standards.intent:
The hospice and palliative care service are responsible for ensuring that appropriate ICT equipment is available and ready for use at all times. There is an accountable, systematic approach to ensuring that cost-effective, safe and appropriate equipment is available to meet the demands of quality patient care.
Managers take responsibility for ensuring that ICT equipment is available and appropriately maintained and that personnel are competent to use it.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures guide the management of ICT equipment are implemented.
criteria.guideline: All key processes that relate to the management of ICT equipment should be identified and the correct manner of performing the tasks documented. The purpose of this requirement is to ensure that important tasks are performed correctly and consistently.

criteria.criterion_text: A designated individual supervises the management of ICT equipment in the service.
criteria.guideline: If this role is fulfilled by the service manager or solo practitioner, compliance will be measured on evidence of effective management of ICT equipment. Where a designated individual has been identified to fulfil this role, compliance will be measured in accordance with the requirements set out in the employee’s position description.

criteria.criterion_text: There is an inventory of all ICT equipment.
criteria.guideline: This does not have to be a separate inventory, it can form part of a single inventory document for the service. All ICT equipment must be included in the inventory (printers, laptop computers, desktop computers etc.)

criteria.criterion_text: All desktop and server computers are provided with surge protection and the server is protected by an uninterruptable power supply.
criteria.guideline: Compliance with this criterion will ensure that data is not lost due to loss of power and that ICT equipment is not damaged, with potential loss of data, due to power surges.

criteria.criterion_text: A documented policy is available clearly describing appropriate back up procedures for electronic records.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: Regular checks are made and documented to ensure that backup has been successful.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: Records are kept of the checking and maintenance of ICT equipment.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: The service has appropriate and up to date virus protection software and firewall protection to ensure adequate security and confidentiality of patient related information.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  Documented evidence will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: There is documented evidence that relevant personnel are regularly trained to use/operate ICT equipment.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.

categories.category_title: Patient record and personal information safety

standards.standard_title: A system for the storage, retrieval, retention and destruction of health records and personal information that meets the need for confidentiality and safety is implemented.
standards.intent:
Policies and procedures as well as managerial supervision ensure the safety and confidentiality of patient records and personal information.
The hospice and palliative care service develop and implement a policy that guides the storage, retrieval, retention and destruction of patient records. Patient records and other data and information are retained for sufficient periods to comply with law and regulation and support patient care, the management of the service, legal documentation, research and education. The retention policy is consistent with the confidentiality and security of such information. When the retention period is complete, patient records and other data and information are destroyed appropriately.
The policy will define:
Levels of access for individual personnel members
The user's obligation to keep information confidential
The process followed when confidentiality and/or security are violated
Secure storage and retrieval of paper-based and electronic patient records and personal information
The protection of records against damage, fire, flood, theft, loss and electronic failure
The arrangements for succession of patient records in the event of closure of the hospice and palliative care service or death of the practitioner
The criteria for selection and method of destruction of patient records and personal information
Personnel members responsible for health record management must have suitable training and experience.
Patient records must be readily available each time the patient visits the service and therefore must be filed in such a way that they are easily identified.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures for the storage, retrieval, retention, destruction and confidentiality and safety of patient records and personal information are implemented.
criteria.guideline: Policies and procedures must include at least a) – g) in the standard intent above.

criteria.criterion_text: Designated individuals are responsible for the storage, maintenance and retrieval of patient files.
criteria.guideline: Only those designated individuals may have access to the patient records. There must be evidence of signed confidentiality agreements with each individual who has access to patient information.

criteria.criterion_text: The filing system allows for incorrectly filed records to be easily identified (for example, through colour coding of the records).
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Storage space for health records is sufficient and secure against unauthorised entry.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Records that have been removed from the designated storage area for use are kept out of public view.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: The retention process provides the necessary confidentiality and security.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL This will be confirmed by observation.

standards.standard_title: The cleaning and laundry service are managed to ensure the provision of a safe and effective service.
standards.intent:
Service managers must ensure that a documented policy is available detailing the cleaning and laundry duties to be undertaken and the frequency with which these need to be performed. Where these services are outsourced a contract defines the details of the service to be provided.
The service manager must ensure that facilities and equipment are adequate for the provision of a safe and effective cleaning and laundry service.

criteria.criterion_text: Written policies and procedures relating to cleaning and laundry duties and the frequency with which these duties are carried out are implemented and monitored.
criteria.guideline: Policies and procedures must detail how the core functions are to be performed. Implementation of these policies and procedures should be monitored to ensure consistent service delivery according to agreed standards of practice. Compliance will be verified by documented evidence of monitoring activities and by observation of implementation of the policies.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that laundry used for patients is washed separately from domestic laundry.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Adequate, secure and well-ventilated storage areas are available for cleaning and laundry equipment and chemicals.
criteria.guideline: Chemicals for cleaning and laundry are safely stored in a well-ventilated, locked cupboard or room out of the reach of patients, children and visitors. There is adequate storage place for brooms and mops. Compliance will be measured by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Mops and brooms are cleaned and dried before being stored.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during personnel interviews and by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: The service manager ensures that cleaning and laundry personnel are appropriately trained regarding waste management, infection control procedures, confidentiality issues and any other relevant matters.
criteria.guideline: Evidence of such training will be required.

standards.standard_title: The food service is managed to ensure the provision of a safe and effective service.
standards.intent:
Service managers are primarily responsible for ensuring that the mission of the organisation is met through the leadership at departmental level. Good departmental or service performances require clear leadership from a suitably qualified individual.
Policies and procedures ensure that personnel receive guidance in the functions that need to be carried out.
A system needs to be in place to ensure that food service policies and procedures are known and implemented.
Policies and procedures relating to the food service include:
a)   Wearing of protective clothing
b)   Wearing of jewellery on wrists and hands and the wearing of nail polish while preparing food
c)   Hand-washing procedures
Food preparation procedures
Cleaning of areas and equipment
Separate storage for raw and cooked food items
Disposal of kitchen waste

criteria.criterion_text: A person who is suitably qualified or experienced manages/supervises the service.
criteria.guideline: This requires an individual in the service who has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for the management aspects of the food service. In small hospice and palliative care services, this will usually be the service manager. Larger services may have a designated food service manager. In some instances, these duties may be shared between the service manager and a private, outsourced company.

criteria.criterion_text: The responsibilities of the manager/supervisor are documented.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be assessed with reference to the position description.

criteria.criterion_text: The manager/supervisor ensures that policies and procedures that address at least items (a) to (g) in the intent statement are implemented.
criteria.guideline: Policies and procedures detail how the core functions of the department are to be performed and should therefore be readily accessible to all personnel. Implementation of these policies and procedures should be monitored to ensure consistent service delivery according to agreed standards of practice.

standards.standard_title: The food service department is designed to allow for the effective storage, preparation and serving of food.
standards.intent: The food service manager needs to work closely with the hospice and palliative care service managers to ensure that facilities and equipment are adequate, and that management is kept informed of facilities which are inadequate, of additional equipment requirements, and the current state of facilities and equipment.

criteria.criterion_text: Where a kitchen is provided within the facility, it is designed to allow for the effective storage, preparation and serving of food.
criteria.guideline: Country-specific regulations/requirements regarding the general hygiene of food premises will apply.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a section of the kitchen dedicated to the preparation of infant feeds, where necessary.
criteria.guideline: Infant feeds should be prepared in a hygienically clean area with adequate provision for infection control measures. This dedicated area can be either a designated milk kitchen or a designated area in the kitchen.

criteria.criterion_text: There are separate hand-washing facilities in the food preparation area, with soap and paper towels.
criteria.guideline: The availability of appropriate, easily accessible and sufficient hand washing facilities as well as adequate supplies of soap and paper towels in the food preparation area will be evaluated.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a mechanism to prevent unauthorised individuals from entering food preparation areas.
criteria.guideline: This mechanism may consist of, for example, no entry notices, demarcations, electronic access controls, etc.

criteria.criterion_text: The temperature, ventilation and humidity levels are adequate to provide for satisfactory working conditions and cleanliness.
criteria.guideline: Natural/artificial ventilation of the kitchen, for example, windows, fans, air conditioners and the availability of a system for monitoring the kitchen temperature daily will be assessed.

criteria.criterion_text: Windows in the preparation area have fly screens or another effective method of fly control is available.
criteria.guideline: Other methods could include, for example, fly traps, ultra-violet insect control, etc.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a fire extinguisher and a fire blanket in the kitchen.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.

standards.standard_title: Basic hygiene measures are implemented.
standards.intent:
Country-specific regulations/requirements regarding the general hygiene of food premises will apply. Certification is usually done by the local authorities who then issue a certificate of acceptability for food premises.
Where food provision is outsourced, the supplier must provide evidence of certification by the local authority in terms of relevant legislation.

criteria.criterion_text: The food service area meets with health and safety regulations.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  Country-specific regulations/requirements regarding the general hygiene of food premises will apply.

criteria.criterion_text: Food handlers wear protective clothing.
criteria.guideline: This may consist of hair covering, apron, overall, gloves and appropriate shoes.

criteria.criterion_text: Protective wear is provided for visitors entering the food preparation area.
criteria.guideline: This will be assessed by the provision of personal protective equipment in the kitchen and notices displayed to this effect.

criteria.criterion_text: Equipment, floors, walls and ceilings are kept clean.
criteria.guideline: Equipment, floors, walls and ceilings should show evidence of regular cleaning, for example, documented evidence of cleaning activities completed according to a schedule, as well as being clean at the time of the survey.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel are constantly reminded of the importance of effective hand washing.
criteria.guideline: For example, posters are displayed at basins, hand hygiene audits are regularly conducted etc.

criteria.criterion_text: Preparation surfaces are cleaned and dried between being used for different activities.
criteria.guideline: This will be assessed during the review of food service documentation and confirmed by observation and personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: There are adequate clean and conveniently placed change rooms, toilets and ablution facilities for food handlers.
criteria.guideline: This criterion refers to the availability, convenience and number of these facilities in relation to personnel complement and the cleanliness of, and easy access to these areas.

criteria.criterion_text: Food handlers have access to lockers for their outer clothing.
criteria.guideline: This requirement will vary according to the size of the facility and the space available.  However, this area should be large enough for the number of personnel and must be neat, tidy and secure.

criteria.criterion_text: An adequate number of suitable refuse containers are provided in or near each change room, hand washing facility and toilet area.
criteria.guideline: This criterion refers to the availability of, and easy access to, refuse containers in the above-mentioned areas, the correct use of these containers and whether they are emptied regularly to prevent overflow

standards.standard_title: Menus are planned to meet patient needs.
standards.intent: Menus are planned by a dietician or other individuals with relevant food management qualifications and/or experience. Where required, cultural preferences are taken into account.

criteria.criterion_text: A suitably qualified and/or experienced person advises on meal planning.
criteria.guideline: This criterion refers to the involvement of a dietician or other experienced person on meal planning and special diets.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a planned weekly menu suitable for different seasons.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required, and compliance will be verified during patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Wherever possible, patient food preferences are respected, and substitutions made available.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: Food products and meals are hygienically stored, prepared and served.
standards.intent: Food is stored and prepared in accordance with documented protocols. High-risk food which may be contaminated, and which may contaminate other food is kept separately. This includes such food as meat, poultry and fish.

criteria.criterion_text: Food which is of a potentially high risk, unprepared and prepared food, are kept separately.
criteria.guideline: Incorrect storage of food can cause spoilage and food poisoning, as well as contamination of other food items. High risk food includes meat, fish, chicken, eggs, dairy products, cooked rice, pasta and prepared salad.

criteria.criterion_text: Separate cutting boards are kept for raw and cooked food.
criteria.guideline: This will be assessed during the review of food service documentation and verified by observation and personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Food is kept for a minimal amount of time after cooking and before serving.
criteria.guideline: This will be assessed during the review of food service documentation and verified by observation and personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Food waste is put in covered containers and removed without delay from places where food is prepared.
criteria.guideline: An adequate number of containers must be provided for the amount of food prepared in the kitchen.  They must be covered, and the cover must be removable without being touched by hand, i.e. foot or sensor operated, with a functional mechanism. Containers should be emptied frequently and there should be no smell of rotting food or evidence of accumulated food waste on the rim, lid or any part of the containers.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a system in place to ensure that food handlers report conditions that may result in the transmission of infection to other food handlers or to individuals consuming food prepared in the kitchen and appropriate action is taken to prevent transmission.
criteria.guideline: The infections which must be reported (for example, diarrhoea, vomiting, throat infections, skin rashes, boils or other skin lesions, eye or ear infections, etc.) the system to be used when reporting them and the actions to be taking following reporting should be developed and implemented.

standards.standard_title: Food is stored under conditions, which ensure security, hygiene and freshness.
standards.intent: Food is stored under conditions which ensure security, hygiene and freshness. This requires the documentation of standards and monitoring the conditions under which food is stored.

criteria.criterion_text: Food is stored at acceptable temperatures.
criteria.guideline: 1.1.1.1 Food is stored at acceptable temperatures. The kitchen should document the acceptable temperatures for each type of foodstuff stored.  Thermometers should be functional and calibrated according to manufacturer’s instructions.  Documented records of temperature checks indicating daily temperature checks as a minimum should be kept.

criteria.criterion_text: Food is stored separately from non-food items.
criteria.guideline: Food related non-food items, for example, pots, pans, crockery, etc., may be stored in the same storage area, as long as there are clearly demarcated, and well separated areas designated within the storage area. However, potentially harmful or toxic items, such as cleaning implements or substances, must never be stored with food items.

criteria.criterion_text: Different types of food are kept separately.
criteria.guideline: Different types of food require different storage conditions. All food should be stored under optimal conditions for the type of food.

criteria.criterion_text: Food is stored off the ground on racks or shelving of an impenetrable material.
criteria.guideline: Storing food off the ground prevents water, dust or other contaminants affecting the food. The material used for the racks or shelving should not allow any seepage of liquid onto other shelves or stored food, should not be affected by food which may decay while stored and must be easily and effectively cleaned to avoid contamination of food items stored in the same area in future.

criteria.criterion_text: Walk-in refrigerators and freezers are capable of being opened from inside through a safety release mechanism.
Stock is rotated.
criteria.guideline: Food that will expire soonest should be placed at the front of the stored items to be used.

standards.standard_title: Where contracts/service agreement exist for clinical, managerial or hotel services they provide for adequate number of suitably trained contract personnel to provide a safe and effective service.
standards.intent:
Where support services have been contracted to outside agencies such as cleaning, laundry, food, waste management, building maintenance, gardening, ICT management, etc., the service leaders must ensure supervision of these services to ensures that they comply with the terms of the contract, meet patient needs and are monitored as part of the quality management and improvement activities.
The service manager identifies the number of personnel required for contracted services and defines the desired education, knowledge, skills and any other requirements needed.
Orientation and induction programmes ensure the competence of personnel before they begin to carry out their functions. Contract personnel must act in accordance with position descriptions and are evaluated in accordance with their assigned responsibilities.
Where appropriate, personnel in the employ of the contractor are made aware of issues relating to infection control, waste management, confidential waste and health and safety requirements.

criteria.criterion_text: Copies of contracts for outside service providers are available to those who ensure they are implemented.
Services provided under contracts and other arrangements are formally monitored and compliance with the contract is documented.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: Contracted personnel are managed as determined in the written service agreement.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: The service manager ensures that contracted personnel are oriented to the service and to relevant policies and procedures.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of orientation to both the service, the position and relevant policies and procedures is required.

criteria.criterion_text: The service manager ensures that contracted personnel participate in relevant in-service training programmes.
criteria.guideline: In-service training must include, for example, infection control, health and safety, fire and evacuation etc. A record must be kept of such training and must include all contracted personnel.

criteria.criterion_text: CLINICAL SERVICES AND PATIENT CARE
OVERVIEW OF  CLINICAL SERVICES AND PATIENT CARE
Hospice and palliative care is provided by an interdisciplinary team (IDT) which consists of or has access to the services of the following members:
Medical doctor
Professional nurse/s
Social worker/psychologist and other members of a multidisciplinary team

A hospice and palliative care service provides holistic care to the patient and family and as such, psychosocial and spiritual care carries equal weight with physical care. These activities are based on goals that will allow the service to be conducted in line with the service's mission and also answer the unique needs of the patient and family. Holistic care is dependent on an interdisciplinary approach, which can be strengthened by access to a broader multidisciplinary team which could include dieticians, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, traditional healers, complementary therapists and, in the case of children, educators.
Hospice and palliative care responds to the unique needs of each patient and family. This requires high levels of planning and coordination.
Certain activities which form the basis of all patient care include:
Assessment, planning and delivery of care
Monitoring and evaluating the outcomes of the care provided
Adapting and implementing care plans according to the patients' and families' changing needs
Standards

standards.standard_title: In consultation with the patient, the hospice and palliative care service provides care that is consistent with evidence-based practice and in accordance with patient needs and values.
standards.intent:
Guidelines are found in the literature under many names including practice parameters, practice guidelines, patient care protocols, standards of practice, care pathways, etc. National guidelines for selected conditions are further provided by the Ministry of Health and Wellness. Regardless of the source, the scientific basis of guidelines should be reviewed and approved by the clinical leaders and clinical practitioners before implementation. This will ensure that they meet the criteria established by the leaders and are adapted to the community, patient needs and the resources of the hospice and palliative care service.
Clinical practice guidelines, including those for children, as well as guidelines supporting gender sensitive care relevant to the patients and the services of the organisation, are used to guide the level of care provided.
Once adopted, guidelines should be reviewed on a regular basis to ensure their continued relevance. Consistency in the approach to the management of care among those who are involved in the care of an individual patient is an important aspect of continuity of care.

criteria.criterion_text: The interdisciplinary team uses current guidelines relevant to the hospice and palliative care service, to assist in the management of patients.
criteria.guideline: Guidelines must be available at the point of care and should include high risk, high volume, high cost and rarely seen conditions. The practitioner must consider national guidelines where these are available. The emphasis in this criterion is on the availability and use of clinical guidelines and evidence of both must be provided for compliance.

criteria.criterion_text: The interdisciplinary team set criteria to select hospice and palliative care guidelines.
criteria.guideline: Effort should be made to identify and adopt guidelines that have been rigorously and scientifically developed, internationally accepted and adapted for local use.

criteria.criterion_text: The interdisciplinary team can describe how they ensure consistency in the management of patients.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be measured during personnel interviews and the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: All patients requiring hospice and palliative care have their holistic needs identified through an established assessment process.
standards.intent:
Organisational policy defines the scope and content of assessments performed by each discipline within their scope of practice and applicable legislation. The purpose of the initial assessment is to identify the patient's medical, nursing, emotional, social, spiritual and developmental needs. From the initial assessment, in conjunction with the patient and family, needs are prioritised, and care decisions are made.
Where applicable, a copy of a medical assessment conducted outside the organisation is placed in the patient's health record. Any significant changes in the patient's condition since the assessment are recorded. The initial assessment is modified for patients presenting with physical, psychosocial or spiritual distress.

criteria.criterion_text: Each patient has an initial assessment that complies with current policies, procedures and guidelines.
criteria.guideline: The initial assessment should include the following as a minimum: Health history Insight into prognosis A physical examination Assessment of pain and distressing symptoms Screening for tuberculosis in line with national guidelines Assessment of activities of daily living Psychological assessment Social assessment Cultural and spiritual assessment Risk assessment for patient falls Identification of patients with specific needs, such as those requiring further nutritional assessments, at-risk patients and families requiring psychosocial care

criteria.criterion_text: A paediatric assessment form is used for the assessment of children.
criteria.guideline: The initial assessment should include the following as a minimum: Health history Insight into prognosis A physical examination Assessment of pain and distressing symptoms Screening for tuberculosis in line with national guidelines Assessment of activities of daily living Psychological assessment Social assessment Cultural and spiritual assessment Identification of patients with specific needs, such as those requiring further nutritional assessments, at-risk patients and families requiring psychosocial care Developmental assessment Review of the under-5 card Identification of the legal guardian Educational history for children of school-going age Identifies family vulnerabilities that could include the facilitation of future planning for vulnerable children Identification of patients with specific needs, such as those requiring further nutritional assessments, at-risk patients and families requiring psychosocial care Risk assessment for patient falls

criteria.criterion_text: The scope and content of the initial assessment is defined for each member of the multidisciplinary team.
criteria.guideline: It is accepted that the scope and content of assessment for each discipline is defined if the service makes use of standardised formats of clinical assessment forms for specific use by different disciplines. The absence of medical notes or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel in the patient record will affect the outcome of the audit process. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Only those individuals permitted by applicable legislation perform the assessments.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit, which will focus on the legibility of signatures and the identification of designations/qualifications. The absence of notes made by the doctor in the patient record, or other relevant clinical personnel other than nursing personnel will affect the outcome of the audit process.

criteria.criterion_text: The findings of assessments performed outside the organisation are indicated in a patient referral letter.
criteria.guideline: When the assessment was conducted outside the service, a legible copy of the findings should be filed in the patient's record. Any significant changes in the patient's condition should be recorded. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Initial assessments are completed within a time frame established by the organisation.
criteria.guideline: Time frames for the initial assessment of patients should be established for each discipline. It is important to note that this criterion does not refer to the length of time that it takes to perform the assessment, but the time within which the initial assessment should be commenced and/or completed. These time frames should be stated in the policy framework. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: All patients are reassessed at defined intervals, to determine their response to treatment and to plan for continued care.
criteria.guideline: Patients should be reassessed according to clinical need. This may be hourly, daily, or less frequently depending on the clinical condition of the patient.

criteria.criterion_text: All assessment findings, including initial and reassessments are documented in the patient health record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients assessed as being at nutritional risk receive nutrition therapy from a suitably qualified person.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: There is an interdisciplinary approach to planning the care of patients.
standards.intent: Documented care plans guide the provision of quality holistic care by the inter-/multidisciplinary team. Planning forms the basis of coordinated patient and family care and promotes communication between members of the care team and the patient/family. Patients and families are regarded as an integral part of the care team  and should therefore have the opportunity to participate in decision-making. Care plans are regularly reassessed to evaluate the response to care provided in line with the patient's changing condition.

criteria.criterion_text: Based on information obtained during the admission process, a holistic plan of care is developed, recorded and implemented for each patient.
criteria.guideline: Those involved in the patient’s care form the multidisciplinary team. The constitution of the team will vary for each patient and may include representatives from the medical, nursing, social work, psychology, occupational therapy, physiotherapy and other disciplines, departments or services.  Information from all disciplines involved in the patient’s care should be considered when developing the plan of care for each patient. Documented evidence that the care plan is implemented by relevant members of the interdisciplinary team will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The care plan is compiled based on the problems, needs and strengths identified in the initial assessment.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Where appropriate, the plan of care indicates a time frame for the expected achievement of results.
The care plan indicates the expected outcomes of planned interventions.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies guide the frequency with which plans of care must be reviewed, and each discipline or service reviews and revises as necessary those aspects of the care plan for which they are responsible.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are referred for further investigation and/or treatment as appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients identified as being at risk of tuberculosis are referred for diagnosis and treatment.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: The organisation implements processes to support the patient in managing pain and other distressing symptoms.
standards.intent:
Unrelieved pain has adverse physical and psychological effects. The patient's right to the assessment and effective management of pain and relief of distressing symptoms is respected and supported. To ensure this right is implemented, the organisation develops processes to:
Identify patients in pain and with distressing symptoms during initial assessment and subsequent reassessment
Educate healthcare providers in the assessment and management of pain and symptoms
Communicate with and provide education for patients and families about pain and symptom management in the context of their personal, cultural and religious beliefs

criteria.criterion_text: The assessment process makes provision for patients in pain to be identified.
criteria.guideline: This requirement should be included in the assessment policy and procedure for the service.  Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients in pain receive care according to pain and symptom management guidelines.
criteria.guideline: There should be evidence that all clinical professionals dealing with the patient’s pain management have contributed to the patient’s record. Monitoring of the effectiveness of the pain management must be recorded. Evidence could include the documentation of pain using an individualised assessment form and pain scoring charts. Pain management should continue with the prescription and administration of medication for pain as well as the follow-up assessment after treatment. These records will also be evaluated. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that the effectiveness of pain and symptom management is monitored.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation has processes to educate health professionals in assessing and managing pain and symptoms.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence of personnel training will be required.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that patients and families are educated about pain and symptom management.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: The organisation develops processes to manage end-of-life care.
standards.intent: Dying patients have unique needs for respectful, compassionate care. Concern for the patient's comfort and dignity guides all aspects of care during the final stages of life. To accomplish this, all personnel are made aware of the unique needs of patients and families at the end of life. These needs include treatment of symptoms, pain management, response to the social, emotional, spiritual, religious, cultural and gender-related concerns of the patient and family and their continued involvement in care decisions. The family is prepared for the patient's impending death with sensitivity.

criteria.criterion_text: Policies and procedures that guide the care of dying patients are implemented.
criteria.guideline: Evidence of implementation can be sought in patient records and by observation of the care process.  Another example would be the manner in which the policy on allowing natural death is applied.

criteria.criterion_text: The unique needs of dying patients are recognised and respected.
criteria.guideline: This includes an assessment of the unique needs of dying children and adolescents.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that the care plan is revised to meet the needs of the patient and family during this phase of the illness.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Personnel provide respectful and compassionate care to dying patients and their families.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient, family and personnel interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Post-death care is delivered in a respectful manner, taking cultural and religious practices into account.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient, family and personnel interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Referral and access to bereavement support is made when indicated.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The organisation has a system for providing support for personnel and volunteers.
criteria.guideline: This can take the form of, for example, debriefing, counselling, group meetings, etc.

standards.standard_title: Patients are informed of the range of services and the processes to access the hospice and palliative care service.
standards.intent: Patients and service providers working in collaboration with the hospice and palliative care service need to know how and when to contact the service to access care. It is reasonable to expect most hospice and palliative care services to offer care during normal office hours.

criteria.criterion_text: When patients register with the hospice and palliative care service they are informed of the range of services offered by the service, opening hours, contact details and after hours’ care arrangements.
criteria.guideline: It is advisable to provide this information in printed form as patients may not remember what has been told. Patients must acknowledge receipt of the information by signing (on the registration form) that they have been informed.

criteria.criterion_text: Where the service has an 'on hold' telephone message, it includes a message for an alternative number to be used in an emergency.
criteria.guideline: This criterion is only applicable to services where the telephone system diverts to an ‘on hold’ message when the line is busy during business hours. The purpose of this requirement is to assist patients who are dealing with an emergency during peak business hours, when it might be difficult to get through to the practice. If the service does not have an ‘on hold’ system this criterion will be not applicable.

criteria.criterion_text: A message on the service’s telephone answering machine, call diversion system or paging system provide information to patients on how to obtain care outside the service’s normal opening hours.
criteria.guideline: The intention of this criterion is to ensure that patients are assisted with the relevant information outside of business hours.

criteria.criterion_text: The service renders services based on the needs of the population, during the hours published.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence can be provided by time cards, duty rosters etc. and can also be verified during patient and personnel interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients (and their families or carers as appropriate) are provided with information on the estimated cost of services to be rendered.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit and/or patient interviews.

criteria.criterion_text: In the case of admission to an in-patient unit, patients (and their families or carers as appropriate) are provided with information on any limitation placed on the duration of stay.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit and/or patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: There is a process for appropriate referral of patients for specialised consultation/investigations at other healthcare facilities.
standards.intent:
In some cases, practitioners refer patients for a secondary consultation to confirm an opinion, to request more extensive diagnostic evaluations that may be available to the practitioner or to have patients receive specialised treatment that the referring service may be unable to provide. The hospice and palliative care service must clearly describe the referral process. The facility may wish to consider the use of a standard referral form which includes a tear off slip for the receiving doctor's response following the consultation.
The referral must contain the following as a minimum:
a)   At least three approved patient identifiers
Relevant history, examination findings and current management
Known allergies, adverse drug reactions and current medication
The name of the referring doctor
The name of the doctor/service referred to
Stated purpose of the referral
Request for feedback following the consultation with the specialist

criteria.criterion_text: There is a documented process to refer patients.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The lines of communication between the hospice and palliative care service, referral hospital and community services are clearly defined.
criteria.guideline: Hospice and palliative care service personnel must clearly describe the referral process, especially where patients are sent to another healthcare organisation for specialist consultation or special investigations and then return to the referring palliative care service for continuation of treatment. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The doctor informs the patient if he/she has any financial interest in the referral service.
criteria.guideline: Surveyors must pay particular attention to services that are offered in the same facility by different practitioners for example, where a general practitioner refers a patient to a radiology or laboratory palliative care service in the same facility.

criteria.criterion_text: Referrals are to specific individuals and/or agencies in the patient's home community wherever possible.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients and as appropriate their families are given follow-up instructions which are provided in an understandable form and manner.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: A copy of the referral note is available in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The referral is made on appropriate stationery or electronically.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The referral contains as a minimum a) – g) in the standard intent above.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The hospice and palliative care service keep a record of replies received following the referral of patients.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided of the system that is used in the palliative care service to record and act upon replies received from referral practitioners.

criteria.criterion_text: There are written guidelines for the referral of emergency patients.
criteria.guideline: Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: Follow-up care based on the findings of investigations/consultations performed outside the hospice and palliative care service are noted in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: At registration, sufficient details are taken from the patient to ensure that the patient and/or family members can be contacted by the hospice and palliative care service when necessary and that the healthcare practitioner seeing the patient for the first time has sufficient background information to provide adequate care to the patient.
standards.intent:
Accurate contact details are essential in order to follow up results, recall patients for chronic disease monitoring and contact patients or nominated next of kin in emergency situations that may arise. Background medical information is essential to the provision of adequate care, and should be obtained in the first instance.
The following details should be obtained at registration of a new patient as a minimum:
Name
Date of birth
Identification numbers
Gender
Current address
Telephone numbers
Next of kin
Who to contact in an emergency
Previous medical history
Previous surgical history
Current medication (prescribed and over the counter medications)
Allergies
Immunisations
Health risk factors, for example, smoking, alcohol consumption, physical activity
Patient/guardian signature

criteria.criterion_text: New patients to the service are asked to complete a form detailing at least a) – o) in the standard intent above.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Patients are asked to update the organisation if their contact details change.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: Patients can obtain advice or information related to their clinical care by telephone and electronic means.
standards.intent:
Where patients, who are known to the service, request information or follow up regarding a condition for which they have previously consulted, and it is clinically safe not to review the patient in a face to face consultation, the patient has access to the relevant information by telephone or electronic communication. The hospice and palliative care service has a documented policy regarding such communication that clearly outlines:
a)      Limitations of use
Positive identification of the patient
Timeframe for a response from a healthcare practitioner
That the patient was made aware of any costs involved
Documentation of the communication in the patient record

criteria.criterion_text: The service implements a policy on telephonic and electronic communication with patients that details at least a) – e) in the standard intent above.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during personnel and patient interviews, patient record audits and by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence of service/patient telephone or electronic advice and information in the patient health records.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The service can demonstrate how it receives and returns telephone and electronic messages from patients.
criteria.guideline: Personnel must be able to describe the process detailing which telephone calls are immediately put through to a healthcare practitioner and what process is followed to ensure that calls are returned timeously. Electronic messages can take the form of e-mails, text messages or messages received on messaging applications.

standards.standard_title: The hospice and palliative care service has a system for the follow up and review of tests and results.
standards.intent:
The information gained from tests and results can have a considerable impact on the choices patients and medical practitioners make in patient care. There may be considerable risk in not following up clinically significant tests and results. There is a system to track all results and where potentially serious pathology is suspected, doctors have a system to track that the investigation is completed and the result is received and acted upon.
To minimise clinical risk, results of investigations are reviewed by the medical practitioner, signed or initialled (or the electronic equivalent), acted on in a timely manner and incorporated into the patient health record.
Many hospice and palliative care services provide the results of investigations to their patients by telephone. The person responsible for giving the results should ensure that the recipient of the information is correctly identified using three patient identifiers so that patient confidentiality is not compromised. Acceptable patient identifiers are name, date of birth, address, identification number, palliative care service patient number.

criteria.criterion_text: A documented policy on the review and management of pathology results, imaging reports, investigation reports and other clinical correspondence received by the palliative care service is implemented.
criteria.guideline: The service needs a documented system for the follow up of all tests and results with a strong focus on risk management. This policy must include that there is documented evidence that every report and result was seen, reviewed and acted upon by a medical practitioner.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient health records contain evidence that all pathology results, imaging reports, investigation reports and clinical correspondence received by the service have been handled in accordance with policy.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record and service documentation audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence of review of the results of procedures and diagnostic tests performed by a medical practitioner.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: There is a system to advise patients of the process to follow up on results.
criteria.guideline: Patients must be encouraged to take responsibility for their own health. The hospice and palliative care service must therefore provide the patient with information about what to expect following any investigation. For example, how long it takes to get the results, the method by which the patient will be informed of the results etc. Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: A documented system to identify, follow up and recall patients with clinically significant results is implemented.
criteria.guideline: CRITICAL  The system must be implemented in such a way that a patient who is not immediately contactable remains flagged for follow up. Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: When patients are informed of results over the telephone, the patient is identified with 3 approved identifiers before the information is given.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: When results are given over the telephone, the result is given to the patient themselves or the patient’s consent is obtained before giving the result to a nominated third party.

standards.standard_title: Patient and family education promotes the concept of taking responsibility for one’s own health care.
standards.intent:
Every patient is offered the information and education he or she requires. All clinical personnel within the service work collaboratively to provide education in a coordinated manner. Personnel collaboration helps to ensure that the information patients and families receive is comprehensive, consistent and as effective as possible.
Education is focused on the specific knowledge and skills the patient and his or her family will need to make care decisions, participate in care and continue care at home. Variables such as educational literacy, beliefs and limitations are considered. Each hospice and palliative care service decides on the placement and format of educational assessment, planning and delivery of information. Education regarding high risk health issues relevant to the local population is routinely provided by the service.
Standardised materials and processes are used where possible.
Information provided by the service may include when to resume daily activities, and information on coping with disease or disability.

criteria.criterion_text: The service offers relevant health education to its patients in a planned and consistent manner which enables patients to meet their on-going health needs.
criteria.guideline: This will be assessed based on the evaluation of the patient education activities in relation to the services offered as well as the individual patient education during consultation for example, lifestyle advice, when to seek medical attention - how urgently and at what level of care, etc. Compliance will be verified during patient interviews and patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Posters and pamphlets regarding conditions relevant to the hospice and palliative care service population are visibly displayed and accessible and routinely provided to patients when appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: There is evidence that family members are taught and supported to provide care for the patient in keeping with the care plan.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews, patient record audits and by observation.

criteria.criterion_text: Patient and family education provided is noted in the patient record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: The service identifies community resources which support continuing health promotion and disease prevention education and has contact details of these resources available.
criteria.guideline: The service must identify those resources that are specific to its patient population. Documented evidence is required.

criteria.criterion_text: There is documented evidence that patients are referred to these resources, where appropriate.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audits.

criteria.criterion_text: Information is provided in a manner and in a language that is understood by those making the care decisions.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during patient interviews.

standards.standard_title: Patient records contain the required information for the relevant condition.
standards.intent:
Medical records are intended to support patient care and should authentically represent every consultation (including by telephone and electronic mail). The clinical record of each patient, whether handwritten or electronic, needs to contain sufficient information to support the diagnosis and justify the treatment provided. It also needs to document the care given and the course and results of treatment. A standardised format and content of a patient's record will help promote the integration and continuity of care among the various providers of care to the patient. The hospice and palliative care service determines the specific data and information recorded in the clinical record, but it contains as a minimum for each consultation:
a)   The date of the consultation
Who conducted the consultation
Reason for the consultation
Relevant clinical findings including documenting the presence or absence of jaundice, anaemia, cyanosis, clubbing, oedema and lymphadenopathy (JACCOL)
Vital signs, including weight and height.
Pain Score
Diagnosis
Recommended management plan and, where appropriate, expected process of review
Any medication prescribed for the patient (including name, strength, directions for use/dose frequency, number of repeats and date medication started/ceased/changed)
Complementary medication used by the patient
All requested investigations and procedures
Any relevant preventive care undertaken
Any referral to other healthcare providers or health services
Any special advice or other instructions where applicable
Follow up instructions given to the patient
Health education
For emergency cases, relevant times are recorded, i.e. time patient seen, time drugs administered, etc.
Abbreviations and symbols are standardised. Such standardisation is consistent with recognised local and national standards.
Patient contact details are kept up to date.
All relevant costs are discussed with patients prior to these costs being incurred and the discussion is documented in the patient record.

criteria.criterion_text: Notes for each consultation contain as minimum points a) – q) above.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Handwritten notes are legible.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Notes are recorded contemporaneously or instantly.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: All abbreviations are standardised in accordance with recognised local and national standards.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patients’ records, including contact details, are up to date to ensure the transfer of the latest information between care providers.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Adverse drug reactions are noted in the patient's record.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: The patient is fully informed regarding the estimated costs of any treatment, investigation, procedure or referral and the discussion is documented.
criteria.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

standards.standard_title: The palliative care service ensures that all notice of death forms are completed correctly.
standards.intent:
Information from notice of death forms is used to monitor mortality trends in the population which in turn is used to inform planning, monitoring and evaluation of health service provision. It is therefore of national importance that good quality information is provided to ensure that health needs are accurately identified and met.
Notice of death forms should contain as a minimum:
a)   Immediate cause of death
All identifiable intermediate causes of death
Underlying cause of death (essential)
Any co-morbidities suffered by the patient which are contributing causes of death (not the underlying cause or intermediate causes of death in Part 1)
No abbreviations are used
The handwriting is legible
Any relevant occupational history is recorded
Specifics of cancers and infections must be provided

criteria.criterion_text: Notice of death forms are completed correctly containing at least details a) – h) above.
criteria.guideline: This whole standard will be scored not applicable where no notice of death form has been completed during the preceding survey period. Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

criteria.criterion_text: Where the death was not exclusively due to natural causes, the death is referred to the police or forensic pathologist and the referral is recorded in the patient record along with the name, position title and contact details of the person to whom the referral has been made.
